notes: Founded 1993 by Tom Siebel; rose to ~45% CRM market share by 2002; acquired by Oracle for $5.8B in 2005; Siebel brand persists as Oracle CRM On Demand. At time of report was already the dominant SFA vendor.

CRM/customer service software; held ~22% CRM market share in 1999; acquired by Nortel Networks for $2.1B in stock (2000); Nortel sold Clarify division to Amdocs for $200M cash (2001); Clarify brand absorbed into Amdocs.

Founded 1990 as ProActive Software; CRM/call center/help desk vendor; acquired by PeopleSoft in $433M stock deal (December 1999); PeopleSoft acquired by Oracle for $10.3B (2004); Vantive brand dissolved.

Internal help desk/IT service management pioneer; acquired by Peregrine Systems (2001) during Peregrine's fraud-driven expansion; Peregrine filed Ch.11 (2002); BMC Software acquired Remedy assets for $355M (November 2002); Remedy Action Request System (ARS) became BMC Remedy ITSM.
Customer service/call center software; headquartered Emeryville, CA; acquired by Siebel Systems for $460M in stock (March 1998); absorbed into Siebel's customer service business unit.
Mid-market CRM vendor; IPO 1994; acquired by Consona Corporation (August 2006); Consona merged with CDC Software to form Aptean (2012).

SFA/CRM software vendor; acquired by Baan Co. (Dutch ERP) for ~$250M in stock (May 1997, concurrent with this report); became BaanFrontOffice Suite; Baan itself filed bankruptcy (1999) and was acquired by Invensys then SSA GT.

Mid-market SFA/CRM vendor; founded 1997; acquired by Interact Commerce (merged with ACT!); Interact Commerce acquired by Sage for $260M (2001); Sage sold ACT! and SalesLogix to Swiftpage (2013) for ~$57M.
One of the earlier SFA vendors; merged with Pivotal Software (Vancouver, BC) around 2000; Pivotal acquired by CDC Software (later Aptean).
IT asset management and help desk software; acquired Remedy Corp (2001); filed Ch.11 bankruptcy (2002) following accounting fraud ($1.35B restatement); assets sold; CEO convicted of securities fraud.
BSS/OSS telecom software vendor; acquired Clarify CRM division from Nortel for $200M (2001); Clarify functionality integrated into Amdocs CES suite.
Boston-based IT market research and analysis firm; authors of this report; acquired by Harte-Hanks (2001); brand and content eventually migrated to Informa Tech/Aberdeen.com.
Listed in TOC of report; customer interaction management; acquired by Pegasystems Inc. for $161M (2010); Pegasystems integrated Chordiant's BPM/CRM capabilities.
Business process and CRM software; acquired Chordiant Software (2010); remains publicly traded (NASDAQ: PEGA) as of 2026.
